summary: Both are the gifted outsider who used anonymity to control the narrative about people more powerful than them — except they ARE the most powerful person, which is the tragedy.
The queen KD left. The one who never forgave him. The one thriving with a 22-year-old MVP while he spirals through burner accounts in Houston at 2 AM.
Quiet menace. Immaculately dressed. Never explains himself. Says everything with his chest. 'I'm Chuck Bass' = 'I'm the MVP' — same sentence, same energy.

He's not Logan Roy because they're both old Texans. He's Logan Roy because he refuses to die. 36 years of ownership. 30 years without a Super Bowl. Multiple kids waiting in the wings. 'Fuck off' speeches at the podium.

show_character: Belly choosing between Conrad and Jeremiah
summary: Travis is choosing between January playoff football and Eras Tour stops every February. Taylor in the suite. The cameras between two universes. Some loves are bigger than the cousin.
evidence: The cultural moment of the decade

summary: Tatum is Regina (the alpha). Brown is Gretchen (loyal, paranoid). Jrue is Karen (sweet, shocking when activated). Porzingis is the one Cady didn't see coming. Four-line lineup. Burn Book = NBA Twitter.
She doesn't want to be at the dance. She is going to win the dance. Wemby in San Antonio is Wednesday at Nevermore — alone in a normal world, slowly winning it anyway.
